title: Finance QCI (T-operator coherence) partial-correlates future realized volatility beyond RV itself: partial_r=-0.215, p=4.3e-14, 67/80 grid configurations significant — CONSISTENT

observation.summary: QCI robustness sweep over 80 parameter configurations (grid over window lengths, modulus, K, FH). Primary statistic — partial correlation of QCI with forward realized volatility controlling for current RV — is r=-0.2154 with p=4.3e-14. 67 of 80 grid configurations (83.75%) are individually significant at the same sign. This is the robustness-verified signal behind the [154] QA_BEARDEN_PHASE_CONJUGATE_CERT.v1 claim that stress is a pumper: QCI captures a component of future volatility information not contained in realized volatility.

parent_cert.claim: T-operator coherence (QCI) extracted from asset return series predicts future realized volatility beyond current RV

evidence.description: Partial correlation: QCI vs future realized volatility, controlling for realized volatility itself
Grid robustness sweep: fraction of parameter configurations with significant QCI effect
QCI (T-operator coherence) retains a negative relationship with future volatility across the majority of parameter grid — sign-robust and statistically robust
evidence.value: 84% grid-significance is strong evidence the effect is not parameter-cherry-picked

evidence.description: Effect size is modest (partial r ~-0.22); the finding is about sign-consistency and beyond-RV information, not large magnitude
evidence.value: script 26 (curvature vs HAR forecasting) separately CONTRADICTS cross-asset — see its own cert when landed

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
